titlestmt.titleproper: Norman Cousins Collection of Henry Seidel Canby
Cousins (Norman) Collection of Henry Seidel Canby
titlestmt.author: Michael L. Forstrom

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday May 8, 2021 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Norman Cousins Collection of Henry Seidel Canby

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Material removed from Binders, 1940-1949. II. Writings of Henry Seidel Canby, 1940-1949.

bioghist.head: Henry Seidel Canby (1878-1961)
bioghist.p: Henry Seidel Canby (1878-1961), American critic, editor, and Yale University professor. Canby graduated from Yale in 1899 and then taught at the university until becoming a professor in 1922. Editor of The Literary review of the New York evening post , Canby was one of the founders and editors of the Saturday review .

bioghist.head: Norman Cousins (1915-1990)
bioghist.p: Norman Cousins (1915-1990), American essayist and long-time editor (1942-1972) of the Saturday review .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Cousins Collection of Henry Seidel Canby. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The contents of each of the four individual binders in Series I. has been preserved in the order it was found. The contents includes both loose items and items mounted on sheets. For preservation purposes, acid-free archival paper has been interleaved around the aging carbon letters from Canby to others and other materials, such as clippings, as appropriate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, drafts and printed versions of writings (box 7), manuscript notes, printed ephemera, clippings, photographs (box 2), and contractual agreements by or relating to the professional activities of Henry Seidel Canby. The collection features approximately 983 pieces of correspondence dating from 1940 through 1949 (boxes 1-6). Incoming letters and typescript carbon copies of Canby's outgoing letters concern professional matters chiefly, addressing publications by Canby during this ten-year period, including critical biographies of American authors Henry David Thoreau (1939) (box 1) and Walt Whitman (1943) (boxes 2-3), his Family history (1945) (box 6), American memoir (box 4), and the collaboration with Robert E. Spiller, Willard Thorp, Thomas H. Johnson and others on the Literary history of the United States (1948) (box 5). Correspondents include publishers, literary scholars and critics, libraries, historical societies, bookstores, family, and others. In addition to the correspondence with the collaborators already noted, there is correspondence with Gay Wilson Allen, Carl Bode, Allen French, Clifton Joseph Furness, Howard Mumford Jones, Allan Nevins, Herbert V. Prochnow, Cleveland Rodgers, Townsend Scudder, Joseph T. Shipley, Stanley T. Williams, and Frances Winwar. Publishers include Farrar & Rinehart, Houghton Mifflin Company, and Macmillan Company.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Norman Cousins Collection of Henry Seidel Canby is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Material removed from Binders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The binders include correspondence and other materials documenting Canby's professional activities during the 1940s. The binders feature approximately 983 pieces of correspondence, including both original incoming letters to Canby and typescript carbon copies of his outgoing correspondence.

Binder [1] contains correspondence relating to Canby's professional work on 19th-century American authors Henry David Thoreau and Walt Whitman, although some other material, such as manuscript drafts and notes, addresses, clippings, and printed ephemera, can also be found. Some letters address specifically the production and reception of Canby's Thoreau (Boston: Houghton Mifflin, 1939) and Walt Whitman, an American (Boston: Houghton Mifflin, 1943). Correspondence also touches on Canby's work as a contributor to the Saturday review . Correspondents include publishers, the Walt Whitman Foundation, and other Thoreau and Whitman scholars. Correspondents include: Gay Wilson Allen, Carl Bode, Allen French, Clifton Joseph Furness, Herbert V. Prochnow, Cleveland Rodgers, Joseph T. Shipley, and Frances Winwar.

Binder [2] consists chiefly of correspondence, but also contains photographs, financial statements, contracts, short texts, and a clipping. In addition to some fan mail, correspondence with publishers Farrar & Rinehart, The Atlantic monthly , and Houghton Mifflin Company concerns works by Canby published during the 1940s, including The Brandywine (New York: Farrar & Rinehart, 1941) and American memoir (Boston: Houghton Mifflin, 1947).

The bulk of the Binder [3] correspondents include Robert Ernest Spiller, Thomas H. Johnson, Willard Thorp, and the Macmillan Company. Letters with these correspondents relate chiefly to work on production of The Literary history of the United States (New York: Macmillan, 1948), to which Canby, Spiller, Johnson, and Thorp contributed as editors. Other correspondents include Stanley T. Williams, Townsend Scudder, Howard Mumford Jones, and Allan Nevins.

Binder [4] also includes correspondence dating from 1940 through 1949, with larger groups of letters with publishers Houghton Mifflin Company and D. Appleton-Century Company relating to publishing projects such as American memoirs and The Book of the short story respectively.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by binder in supplied binder number order.

Writings of Henry Seidel Canby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. features drafts of shorter works by Canby, chiefly essays, as well as research material and drafts of portions of two longer works, American memoir and work on Walt Whitman.

did.unittitle: List of box contents
American memoir , Chapter IX, drafts, autograph manuscript, corrected, and typescript carbon, and miscellaneous pages
American memoir , Part III, "Brief golden age," draft, typescript carbon, corrected and signed
"Beach red," essay, draft, typescript carbon
"Books in war time," essay, draft, autograph manuscript, corrected
"Common sense on the approaching crisis," drafts, typescript, corrected, and typescript mimeograph
"Erskine Caldwell," essay, draft, typescript carbon
"Ezra Pound," draft, autograph manuscript, corrected
["Family history"?], pages 106-112, draft, typescript carbon, corrected
"Footnotes to 1949," essay, draft, typescript carbon, corrected
"The 'Fourth' in our town," draft, autograph manuscript, corrected and signed
"Henry David Thoreau," draft, typescript carbon
"Henry Wadsworth Longfellow," essay, draft, typescript carbon (2 copies)
"Jingle bells! Jingle bells!" draft, autograph manuscript, corrected and signed
"The Middle states: Land of promise," essay, drafts, autograph manuscript, corrected, and typescript carbon
"Peace on earth and how to get it," essay, draft, typescript carbon
"The Shape of things to come," essay, draft, autograph manuscript, corrected and signed
"Stephen Vincent Benét," drafts, autograph manuscript, corrected, and typescript carbon
"Town meeting," drafts, autograph manuscript, corrected, and typescript carbon
"Walt Whitman," bibliographical notes, typescript
"Walt Whitman," research material
"Walt Whitman," miscellaneous drafts, autograph manuscript, typescript, and typescript carbon, corrected
"Walt Whitman and free verse," essay, draft, typescript carbon
Untitled drafts, autograph manuscript, corrected, and typescript carbon
Royalty reports
